sections.name: Galudu

link.alt: Pathfinder #165: Eyes of Empty Death pg. 36
link.name: Pathfinder #165: Eyes of Empty Death pg. 36

sections.text: *Nethys Note: No description has been provided for this creature.*

link.alt: Pathfinder #165: Eyes of Empty Death pg. 36
link.name: Pathfinder #165: Eyes of Empty Death pg. 36

traits.name: Unique
traits.text: A rules element with this trait is one-of-a-kind. The DC of Recall Knowledge checks related to creatures with this trait is increased by 10.

traits.name: Chaotic
traits.text: Chaotic effects often manipulate energy from chaos-aligned Outer Planes and are anathema to lawful divine servants or divine servants of lawful deities. A creature with this trait is chaotic in alignment.

traits.name: Caligni
traits.text: These subterranean people have darkvision, and some have powers to create darkness.

traits.name: Humanoid
traits.text: Humanoid creatures reason and act much like humans. They typically stand upright and have two arms and two legs.

automatic_abilities.name: Death Motes
automatic_abilities.text: When Galudu dies, her body explodes into brilliant motes of magical energy. All creatures in a 20-foot burst take 12d6 force damage (DC 30 basic Reflex save). Galudu's gear is unaffected by the explosion and is left in a pile where she died.

traits.name: Force
traits.text: Effects with this trait deal force damage or create objects made of pure magical force.

traits.name: Light
traits.text: Light effects overcome non-magical darkness in the area, and can counteract magical darkness. You must usually target darkness magic with your light magic directly to counteract the darkness, but some light spells automatically attempt to counteract darkness.

reactive_abilities.name: Opportune Step
reactive_abilities.trigger: A dazzled or blinded creature, or a creature with light blindness, ends its move adjacent to Galudu

traits.name: Agile
traits.text: The multiple attack penalty you take with this weapon on the second attack on your turn is –4 instead of –5, and –8 instead of –10 on the third and subsequent attacks in the turn.

traits.name: Finesse
traits.text: You can use your Dexterity modifier instead of your Strength modifier on attack rolls using this melee weapon. You still use your Strength modifier when calculating damage.

traits.name: Versatile
traits.text: A versatile weapon can be used to deal a different type of damage than that listed in the Damage entry. This trait indicates the alternate damage type. For instance, a piercing weapon that is versatile S can be used to deal piercing or slashing damage. You choose the damage type each time you make an attack.

traits.name: Agile
traits.text: The multiple attack penalty you take with this weapon on the second attack on your turn is –4 instead of –5, and –8 instead of –10 on the third and subsequent attacks in the turn.

traits.name: Thrown
traits.text: You can throw this weapon as a ranged attack, and it is a ranged weapon when thrown. A thrown weapon adds your Strength modifier to damage just like a melee weapon does. When this trait appears on a melee weapon, it also includes the range increment. Ranged weapons with this trait use the range increment specified in the weapon’s Range entry.

traits.name: Versatile
traits.text: A versatile weapon can be used to deal a different type of damage than that listed in the Damage entry. This trait indicates the alternate damage type. For instance, a piercing weapon that is versatile S can be used to deal piercing or slashing damage. You choose the damage type each time you make an attack.

ability.name: Sneak Attack
ability.text: Galudu deals an additional 3d6 precision damage to flat-footed creatures.

universal_monster_ability.name: Sneak Attack
universal_monster_ability.text: When the monster Strikes a creature that has the flat-footed condition with an agile or finesse melee weapon, an agile or finesse unarmed attack, or a ranged weapon attack, it also deals the listed precision damage. For a ranged attack with a thrown weapon, that weapon must also be an agile or finesse weapon.

ability.name: Survivor's Nourishment
ability.text: A creature that eats food conjured by Galudu's create food spell regains 4d4 Hit Points. A creature can only benefit from this healing once per week.

traits.name: Healing
traits.text: A healing effect restores a creature's body, typically by restoring Hit Points, but sometimes by removing diseases or other debilitating effects.

link.alt: Pathfinder #165: Eyes of Empty Death pg. 36
link.name: Pathfinder #165: Eyes of Empty Death pg. 36
